channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Guatemala gives Rubio a second deportation deal for migrants being sent home from the US
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — Guatemalan President Bernardo Arévalo said Wednesday his country will accept migrants from other countries who are being deported from the United States, the second deportation deal that Secretary of State Marco Rubio has reached during a Central America trip that has been focu
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US to seize Venezuelan president's plane held in Dominican Republic during Rubio visit
item.description: The Trump administration plans to seize the second of two planes belonging to Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro's government that is currently in the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NJ lawmakers create new bill to combat impersonating first responders
item.description: WEST WINDSOR, N.J. (PIX11) - First responders can be some of the most trustworthy figures in our communities, but some will try to impersonate them to commit other crimes.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: In their own words: What Trump said about Gaza and how top administration officials contradicted him
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Top Trump administration voices on Wednesday contradicted some of the comments the president made a day earlier about the U.S. taking long-term control of war-shattered Gaza, the possibility of sending in American troops and the area’s residents being permanently resettled.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As case involving Prince Harry's immigration paperwork inches forward, judge considers next steps
item.description: A federal judge is considering next steps in a slow-moving court case that could spell legal trouble for Prince Harry.
item.dc:creator: Ashraf Khalil, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: IRS workers involved in 2025 tax season can't take buyout until after the taxpayer filing deadline
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — IRS employees involved in the 2025 tax season will not be allowed to accept a buyout offer from the Trump administration until after the taxpayer filing deadline, according to a letter sent Wednesday to IRS employees.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 60 Minutes publishes transcript of Harris interview amid FCC investigation
item.description: "We are posting the same transcripts and videos of our interview with Vice President Kamala Harris that we provided to the FCC," the news program wrote on its website.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic senators protest after they say Trump gives Musk's staff access to classified info
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats on the Senate Intelligence Committee are demanding answers after they say President Donald Trump gave billionaire Elon Musk and his staff access to sensitive data and classified secrets as part of their work to overhaul the federal government.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Catholic aid group downsizes by nearly 50% amid USAID freeze
item.description: The group started shutting down programs funded by USAID, which supplied about half of its $1.5 billion budget.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Walz sets special election for seat in suburban Ramsey County that will decide House power
item.description: A special election will fill a seat won by a Democrat in the November election that came open after a residency challenge. The result could return the Minnesota House to a tie or boost a GOP majority.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Justice Dept. official accuses FBI chief of 'insubordination,' tamps down talk of revenge on agents
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A top Justice Department official accused the FBI's acting leaders of “insubordination” in a Wednesday memo in which he sought to soothe anxiety inside the bureau over the potential for a broad purge of agents involved in investigating the Jan. 6 riot at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Scott Turner to lead HUD
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday confirmed Scott Turner to lead the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD), cementing another one of President Trump’s Cabinet choices. Senators voted 55-44 on Turner’s nomination. Sens. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) and Peter Welch (D-Vt.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Scott Turner confirmed as Trump's HUD secretary after pledging to address housing shortages
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Scott Turner was confirmed Wednesday as the housing secretary, a role central to President Donald Trump’s domestic agenda at a time when most Americans say the cost of living around necessities are a top concern.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US officials now say Trump only wants to displace Palestinians from Gaza temporarily
item.description: President Donald Trump’s top diplomat and his main spokesperson are walking back the idea that he wants the permanent relocation of Palestinians from Gaza, after American allies and even Republican lawmakers rebuffed his suggestion that the U.S. take “ownership” of the territory.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mahomes reveals thoughts on Trump attending the Super Bowl
item.description: Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes was asked what it will feel like to play in front of a sitting president.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Petrulis, NewsNation

item.title: US allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: When the president was asked about possibly sending in U.S. troops to Gaza, he said America will “do what is necessary.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Homeland Security's workforce might not be as big as Trump's immigration goals
item.description: Changes to hiring and retention practices grew the department by 19,000 people in four years. But former officials warn that's still not enough for Trump's ambitious policy goals.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Nevada highway projects stuck as 90-day review plays out; Brightline effects uncertain
item.description: Nevada is waiting for a sign from the federal government: Can we count on funding or not? "So, we have pulled in a lot of what we are doing," NDOT Director Tracy Larkin Thomason told lawmakers on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he wants the U.S. to take ownership of the Gaza Strip
item.description: President Trump and Israeli leader Benjamin Netanyahu met Tuesday at the White House where Trump floated the idea of the U.S. taking ownership of the Gaza Strip and redeveloping the territory.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. appears on track to become US health secretary as he wins key Republican senator's support
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Robert F. Kennedy Jr., a vocal vaccine skeptic and activist lawyer, appeared on track to become the nation's health secretary after winning the crucial support of Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Schools poised to be major front line in Trump immigration crackdown
item.description: Oklahoma is moving to require proof of citizenship for the guardians of public school students, while California schools are distributing "know your rights" cards to immigrant families as K-12 campuses become a crucial front line in President Trump's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: President Trump casts blame during briefing on deadly plane crash
item.description: President Donald Trump started Thursday’s press briefing with a moment of silence for the victims and their families after a deadly crash involving an American Airlines plane and an Army helicopter near Ronald Reagan National Airport on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, Fox 5
